title: Add 10 and Ones Within 20
description: Given an equation that adds 10 and a one-digit number (or vice versa), compute the teen-number sum.
condition: An addition equation of the form 10 + n or n + 10 where n is a one-digit whole number.
response: State or write the sum as a teen number (e.g., 10 + 4 = 14).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Relies on understanding teen numbers as 10 ones and some more ones.

title: Compose Teen Number as 10 and Ones
description: Recognize or construct a teen number as a full group of 10 and some additional ones.
condition: A target teen number between 11 and 19 or a visual showing a full 10-frame and extra dots.
response: Represent or interpret the number as 10 ones and some more ones (e.g., 14 as 10 and 4).
label: Principle / Model
example: A full 10-frame and 4 extra dots represent $10 + 4 = 14$.
notes: Central place-value idea for teen numbers.

title: Count Objects up to 20
description: Accurately count a set of objects (dots or fingers) arranged in rows or scattered, up to 20.
condition: A visual set of 0–20 objects arranged in rows, a 10-frame, or finger representations.
response: Count each object once and determine the total quantity.
label: Production / Skill
example: Counting 18 dots arranged in two rows results in $18$.
notes: Involves one-to-one correspondence and keeping track while counting.

title: Identify Full 10-Frame as Ten
description: Recognize that a completely filled 10-frame represents exactly 10 objects.
response: State that the quantity shown is 10.
label: Fact

title: Select Images that Total 14
description: Determine which combination of visual groups composes 14 by recognizing 10 and 4 more.
condition: Multiple image options showing different quantities of dots; task asks for the pair that makes 14.
response: Choose the images that together represent 10 and 4 more (total 14).
label: Principle / Model
example: Select a full 10-frame and a group of 4 dots since $10 + 4 = 14$.

title: Write Numeral for Counted Quantity
description: Record the counted number of objects using the correct written numeral.
condition: A determined total quantity of objects (e.g., after counting dots or fingers).
label: Production / Skill
example: If there are seventeen objects, write $17$.
notes: Assumes knowledge of standard numeral symbols.
